title: holcus sorghum subsp. verticilliflorus
intro: Holcus sorghum subsp. verticilliflorus, also known as broom millet or Indian millet, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Holcus sorghum subsp. verticilliflorus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against chronic diseases. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of powder or extract, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional medicine.
